Title: Host-induced gene silencing of the amino acid biosynthesis gene acetolactate synthase of Phytophthora infestans caused strong enhanced late blight resistance of potato in the field

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Acetolactate synthase; branched-chain amino acid biosynthesis enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential pathway step for pathogen infection; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports pathogen growth and pathogenicity during potato infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Host-induced silencing of PiALS reduces PiALS transcript abundance during early infection and strongly enhances potato resistance to late blight in detached leaf, near-field, and field assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PiALS was selected as an essential amino acid biosynthesis target gene of P. infestans for HIGS. 2. Potato HIGS lines ALS1 and ALS2 produced abundant PiALS-specific 21-nt siRNAs and reduced PiALS expression in the pathogen during early infection (1–3 dpi). 3. Silencing PiALS strongly reduced disease severity and sporangia production and improved quantitative late blight resistance under laboratory, near-field, and US field conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Important during establishment of potato infection; transcript reduced in pathogen at 1–2 dpi in ALS1 and 1–3 dpi in ALS2 relative to control
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive transgene-derived hairpin expression in potato leaves under CaMV 35S promoter; PiALS-specific siRNAs detected in infected and uninfected leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phytophthora infestans infection; detached leaf assay; near-field conditions in Germany; field trials in Minnesota, USA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Host-induced gene silencing in transgenic potato; luciferase reporter silencing assay; small RNA blot; small RNA sequencing; qRT-PCR; detached leaf assay; near-field resistance trials; field trials
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising HIGS target to generate quantitative and broad late blight resistance in potato; can be combined with qualitative R genes for more durable resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study did not directly demonstrate siRNA uptake by P. infestans or direct siRNA-mediated PiALS transcript cleavage; causal growth inhibition is strongly supported but not formally proven. Target metabolite was not directly measured, and the biological output assessed was pathogen virulence/resistance.
